title: Racosperma poliochroum
intro: Racosperma poliochroum, commonly known as the Queensland bottle tree or Queensland ironwood, is a species of tree native to Australia. It has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Racosperma poliochroum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Racosperma poliochroum is often prepared as a decoction or a tincture, and is sometimes combined with other herbs to treat a range of health issues.
taxonomy: Racosperma poliochroum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It is native to Western Australia. The classification of Racosperma poliochroum is further divided into the kingdom Plantae and the phylum Magnoliophyta. This classification system is based on the botanical nomenclature standards. Racosperma poliochroum is a synonym for the species Acacia poliochrouma.
